caseNameFull: BASIC INC. Et Al. v. LEVINSON Et Al.
court: Supreme Court of the United States

Justice White,
with whom
Justice O’Connor joins, concurring in part and dissenting in part.
I join Parts I — III of the Court’s opinion, as I agree that the standard of materiality we set forth in TSC Industries, Inc. v. Northway, Inc., 426 U. S. 438, 449 (1976), should be applied to actions under § 10(b) and Rule 10b-5. But I dissen

485 U.S. 224 (1988)
BASIC INC. ET AL.
v.
LEVINSON ET AL.
No. 86-279.
Supreme Court of United States.
Argued November 2, 1987
Decided March 7, 1988
CERTIORARI TO THE UNITED STATES COURT OF APPEALS FOR THE SIXTH CIRCUIT
*225 Joel W. Sternman argued the cause for petitioners. With him on the briefs were H.

Justice Blackmun
delivered the opinion of the Court.
This case requires us to apply the materiality requirement of § 10(b) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 (1934 Act), 48 Stat. 881, as amended, 15 U. S.
